MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Jason Hain, Esq. (415.947.0797 x.2141; jasonh@evictiondefense.org) of Eviction Defense Collaborative for Plaintiff.
Marcus N. McElhenney, Esq. (no information provided) for Defendant.
Justin Hartley, Esq. (925.337.5623) of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth for Defendant (audio only).

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF ROBERT ROSE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And For Monetary Sanctions:

Motion is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Granted as to special interrogatories 36-38. Defendant's responses will be produced by Dec. 15, 2022. Remaining motion is largely moot as further responses were already produced. Matter is continued to January 26, 2023 at 9:00am, Discovery Dept. regarding issue of sanctions.

Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment (Ccp 473)
Off Calendar Dec-03-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Kathryn C. Klaus; Coddington, Hicks & Danforth, APC; 650-592-5400; appearing via Zoom on behalf of defendant Nina Guralnik.
Jason A. Hain; Eviction Defense Collaborative; 415-947-0797 ext. 2141; appearing via Zoom on behalf of plaintiff Robert Rose.
Nina Guralnik; defendant, in attendance via Zoom.

Hearing held. The court notes the absence of the process server, as well as the "cleaning lady" referenced in defendant's declaration. Defendant's counsel requests that the court grant their motion and vacate the default judgment so that litigation can move forward. Plaintiff's counsel states that defendant avoided service, and requests a continuance so that the process server can attend the hearing and determine if the defendant is in fact the person they served. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANT NINA GURALNIK'S AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT (CCP 473) is granted.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
